title: 10 best herbal creams for neuritis

remedy_properties: Ginkgo biloba: Ginkgo biloba is believed to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which may help alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and pain.
St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum): St. John's Wort has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with neuritis.
Turmeric/Curcumin: Turmeric contains curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with neuritis because it improves blood circulation and reduces inflammation in the nerves. The active compounds in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect and repair damaged nerve cells. By enhancing oxygen delivery and reducing oxidative stress, Ginkgo biloba creams promote nerve health and alleviate symptoms of neuritis, including numbness, tingling, and pain, allowing for faster recovery and improved nerve function.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the nerves, alleviating neuritis symptoms.

Ginkgo flavone glycosides (including quercetin and isorhamnetin): These flavonoid compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect nerve tissue and reduce neuritis symptoms.
Terpenoids (including bilobalide): These compounds have neuroprotective effects, which may help reduce nerve damage and inflammation associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which contribute to their medicinal properties.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are used for their oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to alleviate neuritis symptoms.
Barks: The bark of the Ginkgo biloba tree is also used, possibly due to its potential anti-inflammatory properties and ability to improve blood circulation.
remedy_recipe: Extract ginkgo biloba leaves by steeping 10g of dried leaves in 100ml of jojoba oil for 2 hours.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth to remove any solids and retain the infused oil.
Melt 50g of beeswax in a double boiler at a temperature of 180-200°f for 10 minutes.
Combine the melted beeswax with 20g of shea butter and 10ml of the infused ginkgo biloba oil.
Pour the mixture into a mold and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nerves, which can contribute to neuritis symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and burning pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected nerves, providing relief from neuritis symptoms like pain, stiffness, and limited mobility.

Pain-relieving properties: St John's Wort has been shown to have analgesic and anxiolytic effects, which can help alleviate chronic pain associated with neuritis, such as sciatica or shingles-related pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with neuritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in Hypericum, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in damaged nerve tissue. By reducing pain and inflammation, Hypericum perforatum creams can provide relief from neuritis symptoms, allowing for improved nerve function and reduced discomfort. This natural remedy offers a potential alternative for managing neuritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This terpenoid compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with neuritis.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid phenolic compound, quercetin has anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which can help alleviate pain and inflammation in neuritis.

Nortriterpenoids (especially Amentoflavone): These compounds have been found to have potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective effects, which can help reduce inflammation and promote nerve health in neuritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in hyperforin and hypericin, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them suitable for creams treating neuritis.
Flowers: The flowers contain hyperforin and hypericin, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, useful in creams for neuritis treatment.
Leaves: They are also a source of flavonoids, such as quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, beneficial for treating neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers from a known source and clean them thoroughly.
Mix the dried hypericum perforatum flowers with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the mixture is smooth.
Add 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil and 5 milliliters of aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir well.
Remove the mixture from the heat and allow it to cool and thicken completely before transferring it to a container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric creams containing curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with neuritis.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of turmeric creams can help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of neuritis.
Pain-relieving property: Curcumin in turmeric creams has been shown to have analgesic effects, reducing pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, which are key factors in the development of neuritis. By modulating the immune response and promoting neuronal health, turmeric creams can help alleviate symptoms such as pain, numbness, and tingling associated with neuritis, providing natural relief and promoting healing in the affected nerves.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a curcuminoid, helps with neuritis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing the production of inflammatory cytokines, thereby alleviating neuritis symptoms.

Volitin (Caryophyllene oxide): Volitin, a sesquiterpene, helps with neuritis by exerting anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.

remedy_recipe: Grate 1-2 tsp of dried curcuma longa root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Combine the powder with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Stir the mixture constantly for 5-7 minutes or until the curcuma longa is fully dissolved.
Add 1/4 tsp of beeswax and stir until melted and fully incorporated into the mixture.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the cream cool and thicken for 30-40 minutes before applying.

remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of dried panax ginseng roots and soak them in 500ml of water for 2 hours.
Combine the soaked ginseng extract with 500ml of distilled water and 20g of glycerin.
Heat the mixture to 80°c for 30 minutes and then let it cool.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter with 50g of the cooled ginseng mixture.
Blend the mixture until it thickens and pour it into a container for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom and dry them properly.
Combine 50 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for two to three weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids, then filter the liquid through a coffee filter.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 20 milliliters of the infused oil and 20 milliliters of distilled water.

Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the roots and sediment.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt the wax and butter.

Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Combine 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add the infused calendula liquid to the coconut oil mixture and stir until fully incorporated.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market in the early morning hours.

Mix 10g of grated beeswax with 20g of coconut oil and 10g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Add the powdered peppermint to the melted wax-oil mixture and stir until well combined, allowing it to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Collect high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in amounts of 20g for every 100ml of cream base mixture.
Grind the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground matricaria chamomilla powder with distilled water at a ratio of 1:10 to form a paste, allowing it to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the paste through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused liquid.
Combine the infused liquid with a cream base mixture consisting of 100ml distilled water, 50ml cetyl alcohol, and 10g beeswax, heating the mixture gently until the ingredients are fully incorporated.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry vitis vinifera leaves in a warm place for at least two weeks.
Grind the dried vitis vinifera leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one teaspoon of the powdered vitis vinifera leaves with two tablespoons of coconut oil in a small bowl.
Add three drops of peppermint essential oil to the mixture and stir well for five minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which helps reduce swelling and pain associated with neuritis in the nerves.

Antioxidant properties: The cream's antioxidant properties, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve tissue, thereby alleviating symptoms of neuritis.

Pain-relieving properties: Kansas coneflower contains analgesic compounds like ursolic acid, which helps reduce pain and discomfort associated with neuritis by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.

Barks: The barks of the plant have been traditionally used to treat neuralgia, which is a type of pain or burning sensation that occurs along nerve pathways, making them suitable for medicinal creams for neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Gather echinacea angustifolia dried root extract of 10% concentration.
Combine the extracted powder with distilled water in a double boiler setup.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes at 180-200°f temperature and 15-20 pounds pressure.
Mix the cooled extract with beeswax and coconut oil in a ratio of 1:2:1 by weight.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify within 24 hours.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with neuritis because of their unique blend of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb, Echinacea purpurea, contains compounds that target inflammation in the nerves, reducing pain and discomfort associated with conditions like sciatica and peripheral neuritis. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties soothe and calm irritated nerve endings, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms. By addressing these underlying issues, Kansas coneflower creams provide effective relief from neuritic symptoms, allowing individuals to regain mobility and comfort in their daily lives.

Feverfew creams helps with neuritis because they contain parthenolide, a compound that reduces inflammation and relaxes muscles. When applied topically, feverfew cream can soothe irritated nerves and ease discomfort caused by conditions such as trigeminal neuralgia and sciatica. The anti-inflammatory properties of feverfew also help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, providing quick relief from symptoms of neuritis. Additionally, feverfew's natural antispasmodic effects can help to calm muscle spasms and cramping that often accompany neuritis.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew's active compound parthenolide has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with neuritis.

Antioxidant activity: Feverfew's antioxidant properties can help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which is thought to contribute to the development of neuritis.

Spasmolytic activity: Feverfew has been shown to have spasmolytic properties, meaning it can help relax muscle spasms and reduce inflammation in the nerves, which can provide relief from the pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and relieve nerve pain associated with neuritis.
Flowers: The flowers of the feverfew plant contain parthenolide, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of tanacetum parthenium, measuring out one part by weight for every four parts of carrier oil.
Combine the dried flowers with carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a ratio of 1:4 by weight.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place, shaking occasionally, to allow for active compounds extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Add beeswax and vitamin e oil to the infused oil at a ratio of 10% by weight, stirring until fully incorporated and whipped.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with neuritis because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in affected nerves. The creams' natural soothing properties calm irritated nerve endings, promoting healthy tissue regeneration and repair. Additionally, hops' neuroprotective agents help to shield nerves from further damage, hastening the recovery process. By targeting inflammation and promoting nerve health, herbal hops creams offer a safe and effective way to manage neuritis symptoms, providing relief for those suffering from this often debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal hops creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like humulones and lupulones, which help reduce swelling and pain in affected nerves, providing relief from neuritis symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in herbal hops creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing inflammation.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Herbal hops creams also contain anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative compounds like linalool and myrcene, which can help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, which is often disrupted in individuals experiencing neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving neuralgia and neuritis pain.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and terpenoids, buds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh humulus lupulus leaves in early morning mist and dry them for 2-3 hours under shade.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered humulus lupulus with 20% beeswax, 30% coconut oil, and 50% sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30-40 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before transferring it to glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. The cream's ability to ease inflammation also diminishes pressure on the nerve endings, allowing them to function properly again. Additionally, lemon balm's soothing and calming effects help to calm irritated nerves, promoting relaxation and reducing tension that can exacerbate neuritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, which can alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as pain and numbness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemon balm have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling and ease nerve compression, providing relief from neuritis-related discomfort.
Sedative and antispasmodic effects: Lemon balm's calming and relaxing properties can help to reduce muscle spasms and cramping associated with neuritis, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with neuritis.
Leaves: Contain rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that soothes nerve irritation and reduces swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or low-temperature dehydrators within 24 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm, dark place for 14 days, shaking daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain and filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter within 30 minutes of preparation time.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for neuritis are topical preparations that help alleviate the symptoms of neuritis, a condition characterized by inflammation and pain in the nerves. These creams work by reducing inflammation, promoting nerve regeneration, and providing relief from pain and numbness. The best herbal creams for neuritis include Ginkgo biloba, Hypericum perforatum, Calendula officinalis, Arnica montana, Aloe barbadensis, Urtica dioica, Melissa officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Zingiber officinale, and Echinacea purpurea. Without proper treatment, neuritis can cause chronic pain, numbness, and tingling, significantly impacting daily life, leading to fatigue, decreased productivity, and emotional distress.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to neuritis that are treated with herbal creams are conditions such as neuralgia, sciatica, and rheumatoid arthritis. These herbal creams typically contain a combination of anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving herbs like arnica, devil's claw, and capsaicin, which help to reduce inflammation, ease muscle tension, and alleviate chronic pain.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with neuritis is a blend of arnica, calendula, and St. John's Wort. Arnica cream reduces inflammation and swelling, while calendula cream promotes wound healing and soothes the skin. St. John's Wort cream, rich in hyperforin, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help repair damaged nerve tissue. Together, these creams create a powerful synergy that can help alleviate neuritis symptoms, such as pain, numbness, and tingling, promoting faster recovery and reducing discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with neuritis because of its unique properties that improve blood circulation and oxygen supply to the nerves. The active compounds in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, help to dilate blood vessels and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate neuritis symptoms. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams may also have antioxidant properties that help to protect the nerves from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall nerve health and reducing discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These help by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system, thereby alleviating neuritis symptoms.
Bilobalide: This constituent helps by reducing the severity of neuritis through its neuroprotective properties, which include stabilizing neural membranes and reducing excitotoxicity.

Ginkgolides: These help by inhibiting the formation of platelet activating factor (PAF), a pro-inflammatory molecule that contributes to neuritis, and also by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are responsible for the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for neuritis.
Seeds: The seeds contain ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing in neuritis.

Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are used to create creams that help to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow, beneficial for treating neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Extract ginkgo biloba leaves using 10 grams of dried leaves per 100 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove solids in 30 minutes.
Combine 20 milliliters of the ginkgo biloba extract with 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally to melt the beeswax.
Pour the mixture into a mold and let it cool and solidify completely in 1 hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams helps with neuritis because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. This makes it an effective treatment for relieving the inflammation and discomfort associated with neuritis. Additionally, the creams' ability to promote blood flow and reduce oxidative stress can aid in the healing process of damaged nerve tissues. The natural ingredients in these creams also provide a gentle and non-invasive approach to managing neuritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with neuritis.
Naphthodianthrones (including Hypericin and Pseudohypericin): Exhibit potent neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce nerve damage and promote healing in neuritis.
Quercetin: Acts as a flavonoid antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, and promoting nerve health and regeneration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Hypericum perforatum to make creams for neuritis because they contain a high concentration of hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate neuritis symptoms.
Buds: The buds are used for their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can provide relief from neuritis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers at dawn to preserve their potency and quality.
Combine the dried flowers with 150 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to melt the beeswax and blend the ingredients.
Stir in 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of sweet almond oil to enhance skin penetration and moisturize.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify for 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with neuritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's ability to soothe and calm irritated nerve endings reduces pain and discomfort associated with neuritis. Calendula's antimicrobial properties also help to prevent infection and promote wound healing, which is essential for nerve regeneration. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Calendula officinalis creams provide relief and support for individuals experiencing neuritis, making it a valuable natural remedy for this condition. Its soothing effects also promote relaxation and reduce stress.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with neuritis by modulating immune responses and suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Phenolic acids: These antioxidants neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress and damage to nerve cells, contributing to the development of neuritis, and promote the healing of damaged tissues.
Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that exacerbate neuritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis are rich in anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which help soothe and calm neuritis.

Leaves: The leaves of Calendula officinalis contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to make a strong herbal infusion.
Strain the infusion and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid that will be used for making the cream.
Combine 1/2 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a saucepan over low heat to melt them slowly.
Add 2 tablespoons of the herbal infusion and 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil to the melted shea butter and beeswax mixture.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely before applying to the skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams helps with neuritis because they contain anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated nerves. The plant's active compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. By applying Arnica montana creams topically, individuals can experience relief from pain, numbness, and tingling associated with neuritis, allowing for improved mobility and reduced discomfort. This natural remedy offers a holistic approach to managing neuritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and analgesic agent, helping to alleviate pain and reduce swelling associated with neuritis.
Bornol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its neuroprotective effects and help in managing neuritis symptoms such as pain and inflammation.
Flavonoids: Possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to protect nerve cells from damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that help alleviate neuritis symptoms.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in soothing neuritis pain and inflammation.
Buds: They have a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried arnica montana flowers from a trusted supplier.
Infuse 10 grams of the dried flowers in 100ml of oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Mix 10 grams of the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with neuritis because of their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The gel extracted from the plant's leaves contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with neuritis. These compounds also promote healing and tissue repair, soothing irritated nerve endings and reducing pain. Additionally, the hydrating properties of aloe help to moisturize and calm the skin, providing relief from the discomfort and numbness associated with neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Glycoproteins: These help with neuritis by reducing inflammation and promoting nerve regeneration due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Aloin: This constituent has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing nerve inflammation and promoting the healing of damaged nerve tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain gel with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
Gel from Leaves: It is used as a base for creams due to its high water content and ability to provide hydration and comfort to the affected areas.
Aloe Vera Extract from Leaves: It is a concentrated form of the gel, which is rich in vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients that promote healing and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe barbadensis leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Extract the gel from the leaves using a spoon or a specialized aloe barbadensis extractor for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 cup of distilled water with 1 teaspoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine the extracted aloe barbadensis gel with the mixture from step 3 and heat it gently for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and let it cool and thicken for 30 minutes to 1 hour before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams helps with neuritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herb's active compounds, such as urtin and ursolic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in nerve tissues. This cream can help alleviate pain, numbness, and tingling associated with neuritis, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing irritated nerves. Additionally, its soothing and calming effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can contribute to neuritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: They help alleviate neuritis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, reducing pain and inflammation in the nerves.
Phenolic acids: These compounds help reduce neuritis symptoms by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help protect nerve cells from damage and promote healing.
Sterols: They aid in neuritis treatment by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, which help reduce inflammation and promote the regeneration of damaged nerve cells.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their high content of urtication compounds like histamine and serotonin, which are released when the leaves are crushed or bruised, makes them suitable for treating neuritis.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica contain similar compounds to the leaves, providing them with anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate neuritis symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and pain, making them a potential ingredient in creams for treating neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems in late spring or early summer for best results.
Infuse 1 cup of dried urtica dioica leaves in 2 cups of carrier oil such as olive oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth to remove urtica dioica leaves and reserve the oil for further use.
Combine 1/4 cup of the reserved infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160 to 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 to 15 minutes to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams helps with neuritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The extract of Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, contains rosmarinic acid and other compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in damaged nerve tissue. Additionally, its calming and soothing effects can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and numbness associated with neuritis. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the skin also allows for direct application to affected areas, providing targeted relief.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: Helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with neuritis.
Limonene: Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can aid in soothing neuritic pain and discomfort.
Geraniol: Exhibits anticonvulsant and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate pain and muscle spasms associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high content of rosmarinic acid and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: They are often used for their fragrance and to contribute to the overall therapeutic properties of the cream, although more research is needed to confirm their specific benefits.
Stems: They may be used due to their potential to add a soothing and anti-inflammatory effect to the cream, although this is less documented compared to the leaves and flowers.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried melissa officinalis flowers in a carrier oil like jojoba oil in a ratio of 1:5 for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 30 seconds to 1 minute.
Pour the hot mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams helps with neuritis because they contain the extracts of the Passiflora incarnata plant, which has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of neuritis. The active compounds present in the herb, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with neuritis. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata creams may also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can contribute to the development of neuritis. This natural remedy may provide relief from neuritis symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, alleviating neuritis symptoms.
Isoorientin: This flavonoid has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
Methylflavonol: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties, which help protect nerve cells from oxidative damage and promote healing in cases of neuritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Flowers: They are used because of their high concentration of flavonoids, including flavonols, flavanones, and isoflavones, which are believed to have neuroprotective effects.
Barks: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds that may help in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in nerve-related conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves on a dry sunny morning.
Steep the harvested plant material in 2 cups of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the oil mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Combine the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture with 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with neuritis because they contain the active compound curcumin, derived from turmeric, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with neuritis. The cream's topical application allows for direct delivery of curcumin to the affected area, promoting faster healing and relief from symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations. Regular use may also help to prevent future episodes of neuritis by reducing inflammation and promoting overall nerve health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help with neuritis by reducing inflammation and relieving pain through their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Volatile oils (mainly containing gingerol and shogaol): These constituents aid in neuritis treatment by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system.
Sesquiterpenes: These compounds contribute to neuritis relief by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that help in reducing pain and inflammation in the affected areas.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which provide anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties beneficial for neuritis.

Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make creams for neuritis, as they contain similar active compounds to rhyzomes, offering anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help alleviate neuritis symptoms.

Barks: Barks of Zingiber officinale can be used to make creams for neuritis, as they are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help soothe and calm neuritis-related pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Combine 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/2 cup of shea butter in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 2 minutes at 30-second intervals.
Add 10 grams of dried zingiber officinale root powder to the mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl to remove any solids.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with neuritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm nerve inflammation, reducing pain and discomfort. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, have been shown to modulate the immune system and reduce oxidative stress, which contribute to nerve damage. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Echinacea purpurea creams can provide relief from neuritis symptoms, allowing for faster recovery and improved nerve function. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative for managing neuritis.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with neuritis.
Flavonoids (e.g., Quercetin, Kaempferol): As antioxidants, flavonoids can protect neurons from oxidative stress and damage, potentially slowing down the progression of neuritis.

Alkylamides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the nervous system, potentially alleviating neuritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate neuritis symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe neuritis.
Flowers: The flowers have a high concentration of phenolic acids and alkylamides, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom.
Combine the dried flowers and leaves with 2 tablespoons of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 hours or overnight in a cool dark place to allow infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Mix the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to create a smooth cream.
